methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $5.6B.

data.narrative:
Safe Superintelligence, founded by Ilya Sutskever, Daniel Gross, and Daniel Levy, is a Series B stage AI & Software company valued at $32.0B post-money, having raised $3B+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, Photoroom, and others) and 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to the $32.0B last post-money valuation, implying $60.8B. Peer-median analysis examines four same-stage, same-sector comparables with a median valuation of $3.0B and range of $500M to $5.6B, implying $3.0B. The stark divergence reflects the target's substantially larger scale relative to the peer set.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $2.5B (low) to $69.9B (high), with a median of $31.9B, which aligns closely with the current $32.0B post-money valuation. Key uncertainties include the applicability of peer multiples given Safe Superintelligence's outsized fundraising and valuation relative to comparable Series B stage companies, and whether stage-progression methodology adequately captures frontier AI valuations that may deviate from traditional progression patterns.
